result.stdout:
Description:
  dotnet-template-localizer

Usage:
  Microsoft.TemplateEngine.TemplateLocalizer [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  export <template-path>  Given a template.config file, creates a "localize" directory next to it and exports the localization files into the created directory. If the localization files already exist, the existing translations will be preserved.

commands.payload:
Description:
  dotnet-template-localizer

Usage:
  Microsoft.TemplateEngine.TemplateLocalizer [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  export <template-path>  Given a template.config file, creates a "localize" directory next to it and exports the localization files into the created directory. If the localization files already exist, the existing translations will be preserved.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Given a template.config file, creates a "localize" directory next to it and exports the localization files into the created directory. If the localization files already exist, the existing translations will be preserved.

Usage:
  Microsoft.TemplateEngine.TemplateLocalizer export <template-path>... [options]

Arguments:
  <template-path>  Specifies the path to the template.json file. If a directory is given, template.json file will be searched within the directory. If --recursive options is specified, all the template.json files under the given directory and its subdirectories will be taken as input.

Options:
  -r, --recursive              When specified, subdirectories are also searched for template.json files.
  -l, --language <--language>  The list of languages to be supported by this template. The following language list will be used as default if this option is omitted: cs, de, en, es, fr, it, ja, ko, pl, pt-BR, ru, tr, zh-Hans, zh-Hant
  -d, --dry-run                If this option is specified, modified files will not be saved to file system and the changes will only be printed to console output.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Given a template.config file, creates a "localize" directory next to it and exports the localization files into the created directory. If the localization files already exist, the existing translations will be preserved.

Usage:
  Microsoft.TemplateEngine.TemplateLocalizer export <template-path>... [options]

Arguments:
  <template-path>  Specifies the path to the template.json file. If a directory is given, template.json file will be searched within the directory. If --recursive options is specified, all the template.json files under the given directory and its subdirectories will be taken as input.

Options:
  -r, --recursive              When specified, subdirectories are also searched for template.json files.
  -l, --language <--language>  The list of languages to be supported by this template. The following language list will be used as default if this option is omitted: cs, de, en, es, fr, it, ja, ko, pl, pt-BR, ru, tr, zh-Hans, zh-Hant
  -d, --dry-run                If this option is specified, modified files will not be saved to file system and the changes will only be printed to console output.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information
